definition: The target gate comparand has no input-byte pre-image (pointer-arithmetic relation, computed overflow sentinel, or loop-state value); I2S substitution provides zero traction while its per-execution CmpLog buffer overhead lowers throughput below the baseline fuzzer.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S adds per-exec CMP-buffer cost with no substitution benefit: the gate is a pointer-arithmetic relation (start>=end), not an input-byte literal, so vpc's extra I2S stage yields no traction while lowering throughput relative to plain value_profile.
signature.one_line: Pointer-arithmetic emptiness check; I2S has no literal to substitute

analysis.summary_one_line: After whitespace trim, the token must be a lone sign character (+ or -) with no digits following, so start reaches end.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A byte region parsed as a number where, after leading/trailing whitespace is consumed and exactly one sign char (0x2b '+' or 0x2d '-') is consumed at start[0], start advances to meet end — i.e. a sign-only token with nothing after the sign.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning value_profile seed contains a long contiguous run of 0x2b ('+') bytes (offsets 0x1d-0x2b) feeding the parser; once the sign is consumed start immediately equals end, so line 659 takes the true branch (W shows T=6 F=0 at 659 vs L T=0 F=12). value_profile reaches it via CMP_MAP gradient feedback on the start>=end / isspace comparisons plus havoc, with no I2S stage.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile_cmplog loser seeds embed a sign (0x2d '-') but surrounded by digits and other content (e.g. 'elf -444444440'), so after consuming the sign there are still bytes before end; line 659 always falls through (L T=0 F=12) and the downstream base-parsing/safe_parse_positive_int path executes 24x while it never runs in W (0 hits).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that bring the start>=end and whitespace/sign comparisons closer to matching, preserving the sign-only-token corpus member; the input-to-state delta added in vpc does not help here because the gate is a pointer-arithmetic relation (start>=end) with no input-byte literal to substitute, so vpc's extra per-exec CMP-buffer cost yields no benefit and it loses, leaving CMP_MAP gradient as the technique that distinguishes the winner.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of start>=end at numbers.cc:659, taken only after a sign char is consumed.
Reaching line 659 requires line 657 to be true, i.e. start[0] is a sign character.
The winning value_profile seed carries a long run of 0x2b ('+') bytes in the parsed region.
The loser value_profile_cmplog seed embeds a sign followed by digits ('elf -444444440'), so start never reaches end after the sign.
At line 659 the winner takes the true branch while the loser only takes false.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 659  T=6 F=0  T=0 F=12  if (start >= end) {

evidence_trail.claim: The loser executes the downstream safe_parse_positive_int path 24 times that the winner never enters.
value_profile uses CMP_MAP gradient feedback and has no I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace and per-execution CMP-buffer cost relative to value_profile.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the start>=end-after-sign gate is the only divergence shows value_profile_cmplog resolving as fast as or faster than value_profile (i.e. adding I2S does not hurt), which would refute the claim that the I2S delta is the cause of vpc's loss.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Attributing vpc's loss specifically to wasted I2S/CMP-buffer overhead rather than ordinary run-to-run variance is inferential; the gate has no input-byte literal for I2S to act on, but the per-exec-cost penalty is not directly measured here.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S offers no help because PNG_FIXED_ERROR is an internal sentinel produced by overflow arithmetic with no input-byte pre-image to splice; meanwhile I2S's per-CMP callback overhead lowers throughput, so naive's undirected havoc reaches the sentinel faster.
signature.one_line: Internal overflow sentinel; I2S cannot substitute a computed value

analysis.summary_one_line: A short cHRM-bearing PNG reaching png_handle_cHRM whose redy fixed-point at buf+12 parses to the PNG_FIXED_ERROR sentinel (0x7fffffff).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A small (sub-1.4KB) PNG with a 32-byte cHRM chunk placed before any IDAT/PLTE, where the 4 bytes at cHRM-data offset 12 (buf+12) decode through png_get_fixed_point to PNG_FIXED_ERROR (0x7fffffff), driving the redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR comparison true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive's winning seeds are short (656-1314 bytes), invoke png_handle_cHRM 64 times vs 20, and actually take the L1280 true side 12 times (T=12) while never sinking budget into the IDAT/read-row decode machinery; pure havoc byte/word/dword arithmetic mutators repeatedly perturb the redy field until one execution lands the 0x7fffffff sentinel.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog's blocking seeds are large (738-8759 bytes, several 4902-8771) carrying full IDAT image streams; they pour execution budget into png_read_finish_row/png_combine_row/png_read_IDAT_data (hundreds of L hits, zero W hits), reach cHRM only 20 times, and never produce redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR (T=0 at L1280).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive wins by raw havoc throughput: with no CMP interception tax it runs more executions per second and its byte/Word/Dword arithmetic mutators stochastically hit the 4-byte 0x7fffffff sentinel in the redy slot, whereas cmplog's per-CMP callback + I2S buffer processing cost lowers throughput and its I2S substitution offers no help (PNG_FIXED_ERROR is an internal sentinel produced by overflow logic, not a value present as an input-byte pre-image to splice).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR equality test, true side globally blocked.
redy is parsed from the cHRM 32-byte buffer at offset 12.
Reaching the comparison requires a 32-byte cHRM chunk arriving before IDAT/PLTE.
naive wins the I2S-delta pair over cmplog with strong probability divergence.
naive resolved 9/10, cmplog blocked 9/10 on this branch.
Winner invokes png_handle_cHRM far more often than the loser (64 vs 20).
The winner takes the L1280 true side 12 times while the loser takes it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1280  T=12 F=45  T=0 F=19  xy.redy   == PNG_FIXED_ERROR ||  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds are large and spend budget in IDAT/read-row decode machinery the winner never enters.
naive winning seeds are short (656-1314 bytes).
cmplog blocking seeds are large (up to 8759 bytes).
naive has no I2S and relies on havoc + token mutation throughput.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog wins the value_profile-delta pair over cmplog.
value_profile_cmplog resolved 8/10 with hitcount 4 on the branch.
vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets over cmplog's edge feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no value-profile gradient.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR where cmplog resolves as fast as or faster than naive and value_profile_cmplog, i.e. adding CMP interception does NOT slow reaching the sentinel — would refute both the throughput-tax (pair 1) and the CMP_MAP-gradient-helps (pair 2) attributions.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly see the 4 bytes at cHRM-data offset 12 in the seed dumps (the cHRM chunk lies beyond the 64-byte window), so the claim that the redy field decodes to exactly 0x7fffffff is inferred from the L1280 true-side hitcounts rather than observed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S adds per-execution CmpLog-buffer overhead but provides no comparison-solving benefit on this loop-state branch (not gated by an exact comparand in input bytes); the overhead lowers throughput relative to plain value_profile, which reaches the state via havoc.
signature.one_line: IDAT loop-state check; I2S overhead taxes throughput with no traction

analysis.summary_one_line: A valid PNG whose IDAT carries extra compressed bytes past Z_STREAM_END, reached by deep inflate-loop iteration under ordinary havoc throughput.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same condition: a valid PNG whose IDAT stream leaves avail_in>0 or idat_size>0 after Z_STREAM_END, taking the True branch at L4245. The discriminating bytes are header/IDAT-size fields (offsets 0x11 and 0x17), not a magic-constant CMP that I2S could substitute.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 8/10 using naive's havoc stack (no I2SRandReplace). Three of its four winning seeds carry varied 0x11 (00/01/02) and 0x17 (45/46) bytes, exercising the extra-data path with ordinary mutation throughput rather than I2S substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile_cmplog adds a per-execution CmpLog buffer plus the I2SRandReplace stage on top of value_profile. The blocker is not gated by an exact integer/string comparand that I2S can copy from input to state, so I2S provides no traction here; instead its per-execution CMP-record overhead lowers throughput and it resolves 0/10 (blocked 10/10, avg blocked 23.10h).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner value_profile is enabled by havoc-only byte mutation reaching the loop-deep extra-data state. The loser value_profile_cmplog is penalized by its I2S/CmpLog overhead, which yields no comparison-solving benefit on this non-CMP-gated branch while taxing throughput.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the True branch of an OR test for leftover IDAT input after the deflate stream ends.
The winning side is taken only when extra compressed data remains, triggering the benign-error path at L4246.
naive resolves 10/10 and is the winner over naive_ctx via the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx loses, resolving only 1/10 and staying blocked 9/10.
naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The loser invokes the enclosing IDAT function ~20x more often than the winner, showing budget burned re-traversing the inflate loop rather than resolving the branch.
value_profile resolves 8/10 and is the winner over value_profile_cmplog via the I2S delta.
value_profile_cmplog loses, resolving 0/10 and blocked 10/10.
value_profile uses naive's havoc + token stack with no I2SRandReplace, so the winner relies on plain mutation.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace plus a per-execution CMP buffer on top of value_profile.
The discriminating input bytes are header/IDAT-size fields at offsets 0x11 and 0x17, where the winner shows variety and the loser is uniform.
The naive winning seed keeps the PNG signature and IHDR intact, mutated only by havoc operators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=7bc940e72a5dbe0c, size=8832 bytes, fuzzer=naive, trial=1, discovered_at=12240s, mutation_op=BytesDeleteMutator,BytesRandInsertMutator):

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness reproducing the deep IDAT inflate loop with leftover compressed bytes in which naive_ctx and value_profile_cmplog resolve the extra-data branch as fast as or faster than naive/value_profile (i.e. context-coverage and I2S overhead impose no throughput/dilution tax).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The throughput/coverage-dilution tax is inferred from invocation-count divergence and mechanism descriptions; the 0x11/0x17 byte-diff is a correlate of the extra-data condition rather than a directly verified dataflow cause of the L4245 OR test.
